channel.title: lisp-code on mahmood
channel.description: Recent content in lisp-code on mahmood

item.title: working with graphs in common lisp
item.description: my initial approach for simplicity ill represent a graph as a list of cons’s, if we are dealing with a weighted graph, we use the second item in the cons as a cons with the first item being the node and the second being the weight i implemented the helper functions node-weight and node for this purpose an edge is written/stored in the format '(<src> . (<dest> . <weight>)) or simply '(<src> .

item.title: convolutional neural network
item.description: citations (Peter Norvig, Stuart J. Russell, 2020) (Jianxin Wu, 2017) (Zhifei Zhang, 2016) and countless other resources that helped me form a good understanding of the topics related to conv networks motivation an image cannot be thought of as a simple vector of input pixel values, primarily because adjacency of pixels in an image matters. simple multilayer perceptrons arent suited for images because adjacent neurons in the same layer arent really connected or affected by each other, which isnt the case in images.
